content:
5 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Photo by Clarissa Watson on UnsplashLogging is one of the first things developers add to an application.
print("User logged in");Then:
print(response.body);And before long:
print(token);Everything works.
Debugging becomes easier.
Problems are solved faster.
But here’s the problem:
Logs often outlive the debugging session.
Many production security incidents aren’t caused by hackers breaking encryption or bypassing authentication.
They’re caused by something much simpler:
Sensitive information accidentally written to logs.
Passwords.
JWT tokens.
Email addresses.
Phone numbers.
Credit card details.
Entire API responses.
Once logged, this information can appear in crash reports, analytics platforms, monitoring systems, developer consoles, and support tools.
In this article, we’ll learn why secure logging matters, what should never be logged, and how to…
